page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.medium is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.c2 family. It is configured with 16 dedicated physical CPU cores and 16 vCPUs, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio with no hyperthreading. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor.

This server type does not include bundled local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. There are no integrated GPUs, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to memory, the hardware profile is structured for compute-heavy tasks that require dedicated physical cores but have low memory footprints. This resource density makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for pure CPU-bound workloads where paying for excess memory is unnecessary. Tradeoffs include the complete lack of local storage and a highly constrained memory-to-CPU ratio. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, CPU-bound microservices, and parallel computing tasks with low memory requirements.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c2.medium is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance within the ecs.c2 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical CPU cores and 16 vCPUs, establishing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio without hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads that do not require large memory allocations. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for pure CPU-bound tasks by eliminating the overhead of unused memory. It is best suited for batch processing, parallel computing, and lightweight, CPU-heavy microservices.
